ITC1000: Computer Applications. Covers basic computer productivity tools including file organization, word processing, spreadsheets, and presentations. Requires a Windows environment.
ITC1200: Operating Systems Concepts. Introduces structure and organization of operating systems. Compares characteristics of Windows and UNIX, covering general computer organization and configuration.
ITC2000: Principles of Systems Analysis and Design. Covers methodologies, models, and tools used in modern system development, including project life-cycle models, use-case analysis, business rules, and UML modeling.
ITC2016: End-User Data Analysis Tools. Focuses on acquiring and analyzing data using advanced spreadsheet tools and end-user database software. Requires basic word processing and spreadsheet skills.
ITC2050: Designing the User Experience. Studies UX design theory and practice. Covers human information processing subsystems, interface design principles, and usability metrics and evaluation methods.
ITC2100: Introduction to Programming (Java). Hands-on first programming course for those with no prior experience. Covers core Java fundamentals.
ITC2200: Introduction to Networking. Covers TCP/IP, OSI model, routing protocols, switching, and foundational network architecture concepts.
ITC2300: Database Management Systems. Covers relational database design, SQL, normalization, and data modeling for business applications.
ITC2400: Web Design and Development. Introduces HTML, CSS, and web design fundamentals for building functional, user-friendly websites.
ITC3100: Advanced Applications Development (Android). Focuses on building complex Android applications that address business problems, including database interfaces and session management.
ITC3150: Database Websites. Covers integrating relational databases into webpages using open-source tools. Emphasizes querying, updating, and managing databases programmatically.
ITC3220: Mobile and Wireless Networking. Covers wireless network protocols, cellular platforms, mobile operating systems, and wireless security issues.
ITC3250: UNIX Systems Administration. Covers day-to-day management of UNIX systems including administration tasks, scripting, and system operations.
ITC3400: Web Design and Multimedia. Examines multimedia components — text, audio, graphics, video, animation — and their application in website user interfaces.
ITC3500: IT Project Management. Covers tools and techniques for managing IT projects including planning, scheduling, budgeting, and PERT/CPM/Gantt methods.
ITC4200: Network Security. Explores computer security theory and practice. Topics include cryptography, firewalls, VPNs, biometrics, and Internet protocols such as SSL, IPsec, and SSH.
ITC4600: Information Security Management. Covers management issues in information security including asset classification, personnel security, business continuity, and compliance.
ITC6460: Cloud Analytics. Introduces techniques and tools for extracting information from massive datasets via cloud platforms. Covers cloud migration, business intelligence, and data harvesting from social media and e-commerce sources.
ITC6480: Amazon Web Service (AWS) Cloud Architecting. Covers advanced AWS cloud computing topics to develop expertise in cloud architecture and deployment.
CET2100: Essentials of Computer Organization. Covers basic computer architecture, CPU design, memory management, I/O methods, instruction formats, and an introduction to operating systems.
CET2200: Data Structures and Algorithms. Covers design, analysis, and implementation of data structures and algorithms using object-oriented programming. Topics include arrays, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs.
CET2210: Industrial Robotics. Introduces robotics concepts including frame geometry, coordinate systems, control systems, programming, and safety. Covers operational programming and teach-pendant operation.
CET3100: Computer Networking and Communications Technology. Covers technologies, protocols, and techniques for designing, installing, maintaining, and monitoring computer networks.
CET4210: Robotics. Studies deployment of robots in manufacturing environments. Covers design, integration, and operation of robotic systems including sensors, vision cameras, and mechanical-software interaction.
ALY2010: Probability Theory and Introductory Statistics. Introduces statistics for data analytics. Topics include frequency distributions, regression, correlation, ANOVA, and use of statistical software such as SPSS.
ALY2100: Introduction to Programming for Data Analytics. Hands-on first programming course focused on data analytics applications. No prior programming experience required.
ALY5015: Intermediate Analytics. Builds on core analytics foundations to explore data correction, statistical significance, correlation, regression, ANOVA, GLM, and logistic regression using R.
ALY5030: Data Warehousing and SQL. Covers relational database design, SQL querying, and data warehousing concepts for analytical workflows.
ALY5050: Introduction to Enterprise Analytics. Covers forecasting, simulation, linear programming, optimization, and Python. Emphasizes end-to-end analytics from data management through strategy development.
ALY6000: Foundations of Analytics. Introduces core analytics concepts, tools, and frameworks used for data-driven decision making in professional environments.
ALY6010: Exploratory Data Analysis. Covers statistical summarization and visualization of datasets. Introduces R for data analysis and pattern discovery.
ALY6040: Data Mining. Covers data mining techniques including rule-based learning, decision trees, clustering, and association-rule mining. Includes data visualization for pattern interpretation.
ALY6060: Decision Support and Business Intelligence. Introduces business analytics concepts and technologies including CRM analytics, OLAP, OBIEE, IBM Cognos, Tableau, and QlikView.
ALY6070: Communication and Visualization for Data Analytics. Covers data visualization and communication theory. Project-based course applying design concepts to make quantitative data understandable and actionable.
ALY6080: Integrated Experiential Capstone. Applied capstone project where students develop and deliver analytics solutions to real-world industry problems.
MGT1100: Introduction to Business. Covers key external factors influencing business development — political, economic, legal, social, and technological — and explores internal management functions including marketing, strategy, finance, and operations.
MGT2100: Principles of Management. Examines globalization, workforce changes, and technological shifts. Covers planning, organizing, leading, and controlling as core managerial functions.
MGT2210: Information within the Enterprise. Addresses information management and IT systems in business operations. Case-driven course focused on leveraging IM for competitive advantage.
MGT3220: International Business. Covers principles and practices of international business. Examines legal, political, economic, and cultural facets of the international management environment.
MGT4210: Project Management. Covers analytical and people-management tools for project success. Topics include feasibility, scheduling, quality criteria, budgeting, and PMI best practices.
MGT4220: Innovation and Change Management. Covers principles, tools, and methods for implementing organizational change and innovation effectively.
MGT6100: Leadership and Organizational Behavior. Examines leadership theory, group dynamics, motivation, and organizational culture in the context of managing teams and driving performance.
MGT6200: Business Strategy and Competitive Advantage. Covers strategic planning frameworks, competitive analysis, and decision making for organizational direction and resource allocation.
HRM2320: Human Resources Management. Covers staffing, employee relations, motivation, legal considerations, and current HR issues in competitive organizational environments.
HRM6005: Creating a High-Performance Organization. Covers strategic HRM choices, motivation, leadership, team building, corporate culture, and decision-making dynamics in growing organizations.
HRM6010: Compensation and Benefits. Examines merit and incentive plans, wage structures, and flexible benefits design. Focuses on compensation strategy for attracting and retaining talent.
HRM6015: Introduction to Human Resources Management. Covers scope, vocabulary, and strategic context of HRM. Examines competencies for effective human capital management.
HRM6025: Talent Acquisition and Workforce Planning. Covers recruitment strategy, selection processes, and workforce forecasting aligned to organizational goals.
HRM6042: Employment Law and Compliance. Covers key employment legislation and regulatory frameworks governing HR practice in the United States.
AAI5015: Mathematical Concepts for AI. Reviews foundational math for AI studies including linear algebra, probability, statistics, and calculus as applied to machine learning algorithms.
AAI5025: Python for AI. Covers Python programming fundamentals and their significance in artificial intelligence applications, including data structures and object-oriented programming.
AAI5035: Research Methods and Scientific Writing. Examines research methodologies and scientific writing for AI. Covers literature reviews, research paper drafting, and critical evaluation of AI studies.
AAI6600: Applied Artificial Intelligence. Introduces AI history and foundational principles. Covers search algorithms, knowledge representation, reasoning, planning, probabilistic learning, and machine learning basics.
AAI6610: Applied Machine Learning. Covers advanced supervised and unsupervised learning, deep learning architectures, reinforcement learning, and domain-specific ML applications.
AAI6620: Natural Language Processing. Explores NLP techniques and their applications in AI systems. Covers text processing, language models, and real-world NLP use cases.
AAI6700: Computer Vision. Covers image processing, object detection, and computer vision techniques applied in AI systems across various industries.
AAI6900: AI for Autonomous Systems. Explores AI techniques — machine learning, computer vision, reinforcement learning — as applied to autonomous systems. Covers perception, planning, control, and human-machine interaction.
EAI5000: Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence. Introduces AI theory and algorithms including heuristic search, knowledge representation, predicate calculus, automated deduction, and machine learning basics.
EAI6000: Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence (Graduate). Covers foundational AI problems, heuristic search, game trees, predicate calculus, problem solving, planning, and an introduction to machine learning.
EAI6010: Applications of Artificial Intelligence. Explores AI applications in finance, healthcare, education, and transportation. Topics include neural networks, NLP, and cybersecurity implications.
EAI6020: AI System Technologies. Covers systems technologies used in AI including data visualization, large-scale file systems, SQL, and data ingestion and transformation pipelines.
EAI6100: Data Analysis for Artificial Intelligence. Covers instrumental methods of data analysis and modern analytical techniques as applied to artificial intelligence across professional fields.
EAI6120: AI Communication and Visualization. Covers information design and data visualization concepts including exploratory visualizations, dashboards, spatial data, and ethical considerations of communicating AI-driven insights.
EAI6980: Integrated Experiential Capstone. Applied capstone where students develop and deliver AI projects addressing real-world organizational problems using skills from across the program.
DGM6105: Digital Media Foundations. Introduces core digital media concepts, tools, and workflows used across animation, video, interactive design, and storytelling.
DGM6108: Interactive Design. Covers principles and practice of interactive media design. Lab component provides hands-on tutorials and problem-solving exercises.
DGM6122: Foundations of Digital Storytelling. Introduces character and story development across digital media formats including text, storyboarding, sound, moving image, and interactive environments.
DGM6140: Sound Design. Explores history, theory, and practice of sound design including creation of aural environments, special effects, dialogue, and music for various media.
DGM6400: Game Design Fundamentals. Covers basic game design principles through board games, card games, and video game prototyping. Develops graphic communication, rules logic, and basic programming logic skills.
DGM6403: Game Engine Fundamentals. Applies game design principles to multiplatform development and rapid prototyping using industry-standard game engines.
DGM6405: Game Development. Advanced course in game development covering full production pipeline from concept through deployment.
DGM6450: Animation Basics. Explores animation processes and techniques. Covers historical survey of animation and hands-on development of concepts using computer tools.
DGM6451: Web Development. Covers intermediate to advanced web development including client-side interactions with AJAX, JavaScript, PHP, and MySQL, and client/server architecture.
DGM6501: Web Creation Boot Camp. Intensive workshop covering basic web design including HTML, CSS, image preparation, and site development using an industry-standard text editor.
DGM6506: Introduction to Digital Video. Covers video editing and compression techniques using industry-standard software for web and interactive media production.
DGM6510: 3D Modeling. Introduces three-dimensional computer animation fundamentals including modeling, rigging, and rendering techniques.
DGM6540: Compositing. Covers compositing and special effects techniques including green screen production, combining live-action footage with computer-generated environments and characters.
DGM6545: Documentary and Nonfiction Production. Covers research, story structure, and production skills for documentary filmmaking from preproduction through final cut.
HMG1100: Introduction to Health Management. Introduces the healthcare industry, its organizational structures, and the role of management in health services organizations.
HMG2100: Healthcare Operations. Covers operations management planning and execution in hospitals and other health services organizations.
HMG2110: Health Law and Regulation. Examines healthcare law and regulation, including medical malpractice, liability, risk management, and current ethical and legal dilemmas in medicine.
HMG3210: Health Informatics. Covers information systems in healthcare including clinical applications, electronic health records, decision support systems, and security and confidentiality.
HMG3220: Risk Management and Quality Assurance. Explores quality management in healthcare including legislative mandates, credentialing, performance monitoring, and quality improvement methods.
HMG4210: Healthcare Policy. Examines healthcare policy including economics, politics, insurance, Medicare, Medicaid, managed care, and comparative international health systems.
HMG6110: Organization, Administration, Financing, and History of Healthcare. Provides historical and economic context for the current healthcare system, covering integrated delivery systems and political drivers.
TCC2200: Introduction to Technical Writing. Covers audience analysis, content research, document planning, and designing technical content for print, web, and instructional media audiences.
TCC3450: Writing for the Web. Covers web writing styles, information architecture, navigation, labeling, and usability testing. Emphasizes how web readers scan rather than read.
TCC6480: Instructional Design for Technical Communicators. Covers instructional design frameworks for technical writers, including audience analysis, content development, and adult learning strategies.
LDR1200: Introduction to Leadership. Introduces leadership theory, styles, and foundational competencies for leading teams and organizations.
LDR3400: Evidence-Based Leadership and Decision Making. Examines evidence-based leadership components including decision models, ethical problem solving, and using data to align organizational goals.
LDR6100: Leadership Theory and Practice. Covers major leadership frameworks and their application to organizational challenges, team dynamics, and strategic direction.
LDR6110: Organizational Change Leadership. Covers organizational transformation frameworks and competencies for leading change, designing a vision, and managing transitions from current to desired states.
LDR6190: Change Management Consulting. Applies organizational change theory to real consulting engagements. Students serve as change agents for live organizations and develop a personal change leadership model.
